proposed_relations.description: Manhattan/Bronx SBS improvements are part of the LaGuardia Airport Access Alternatives Analysis project

evidence_refs.source_quote: Limited stops Dedicated bus lanes Transit signal priority Off-board fare payment Luggage racks

rationale: Block p001_c0016 lists the improvement features for the Manhattan/Bronx SBS routes under the 'Proposed New Routes' section of the LaGuardia Airport Access Alternatives Analysis handout, confirming these treatments belong to project_lga-access-aa.

evidence_refs.source_quote: Limited stops Dedicated bus lanes Transit signal priority Off-board fare payment Luggage racks

LaGuardia Airport (LGA) is the only New York area airport not served by rapid transit. Local buses serving the airport are slowed by long passenger boarding times and by operating in mixed traffic on local streets. In May 2011 the LaGuardia Airport Access Alternatives Analysis began with the purpose of developing faster and more reliable transit service to LGA.

quarantine.note: None of the cited blocks explicitly state that the Manhattan/Bronx SBS improvements are part of the LaGuardia Airport Access Alternatives Analysis project. p001_c0016 just lists features, p001_c0007 just says 'Proposed New Routes' without identifying what project proposed them, and p001_c0003 describes the AA project but does not mention these specific treatments. The connection relies on document structure and section nesting, which is co-occurrence in a document — explicitly not a relation per review rules.
